diff --git a/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/router.py b/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/router.py
index 02fcded..a65964b 100644
--- a/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/router.py
+++ b/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/router.py
@@ -55,6 +55,7 @@ from agent.integrations.quickbooks.schemas import (
 from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import (
     build_auth_url,
     disconnect,
+    get_state_context,
     get_company_info,
     get_connection_status,
     handle_oauth_callback,
@@ -67,6 +68,11 @@ logger = logging.getLogger("loangen-qb-router")
 
 router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/v1/quickbooks", tags=["QuickBooks"])
 
+
+def _invite_redirect_url(invite_token: str, *, success: bool) -> str:
+    base = f"{settings.frontend_url}/start/invite/{invite_token}/advisor"
+    return _with_query_param(base, "qb_success" if success else "qb_error", "1")
+
 def _with_query_param(url: str, key: str, value: str) -> str:
     """
     Return `url` with query param (key=value) set if not already present.
@@ -111,6 +117,8 @@ async def qb_status(
     ),
 )
 async def qb_connect(
+    flow: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Optional flow context (invite/default)"),
+    invite_token: str | None = Query(default=None, description="Invite token when flow=invite"),
     user=Depends(get_current_user),
 ) -> QBConnectURLResponse:
     """
@@ -123,6 +131,8 @@ async def qb_connect(
         4. Intuit redirects to /oauth/callback — the connection is stored automatically.
         5. Your browser will be redirected to the QUICKBOOKS_OAUTH_SUCCESS_REDIRECT URL.
     """
+    if flow == "invite" and invite_token:
+        return build_auth_url(str(user.id), flow="invite", invite_token=invite_token)
     return build_auth_url(str(user.id))
 
 
@@ -152,19 +162,28 @@ async def qb_callback(
     On success: redirects to QUICKBOOKS_OAUTH_SUCCESS_REDIRECT (frontend URL).
     On failure: redirects to QUICKBOOKS_OAUTH_ERROR_REDIRECT.
     """
+    state_context = get_state_context(state)
+    flow = str(state_context.get("flow") or "").strip().lower()
+    invite_token = str(state_context.get("invite_token") or "").strip()
     try:
-        conn = await handle_oauth_callback(
+        _conn, state_context = await handle_oauth_callback(
             state=state,
             code=code,
             realm_id=realm_id,
         )
         logger.info("QB OAuth callback success.")
-        # Frontend uses qb_success=1 to auto-trigger the initial sync and show the sync loader.
-        redirect_url = _with_query_param(settings.quickbooks_oauth_success_redirect, "qb_success", "1")
+        if flow == "invite" and invite_token:
+            redirect_url = _invite_redirect_url(invite_token, success=True)
+        else:
+            # Frontend uses qb_success=1 to auto-trigger the initial sync and show the sync loader.
+            redirect_url = _with_query_param(settings.quickbooks_oauth_success_redirect, "qb_success", "1")
         return RedirectResponse(url=redirect_url)
     except Exception as exc:
         logger.error("QB OAuth callback failed: %s", exc)
-        redirect_url = _with_query_param(settings.quickbooks_oauth_error_redirect, "qb_error", "1")
+        if flow == "invite" and invite_token:
+            redirect_url = _invite_redirect_url(invite_token, success=False)
+        else:
+            redirect_url = _with_query_param(settings.quickbooks_oauth_error_redirect, "qb_error", "1")
         return RedirectResponse(url=redirect_url)
 
 
diff --git a/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/service.py b/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/service.py
index 234c66c..29f4fb3 100644
--- a/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/service.py
+++ b/loangen-agent/agent/integrations/quickbooks/service.py
@@ -96,14 +96,19 @@ POINT_IN_TIME_REPORTS = {"BalanceSheet", "AgedReceivables", "AgedPayables"}
 # The HMAC is computed with the JWT secret so it cannot be forged.
 
 
-def _build_state(user_id: str) -> str:
+def _build_state(user_id: str, *, flow: Optional[str] = None, invite_token: Optional[str] = None) -> str:
     """
     Build a signed state token that embeds the user_id.
 
     Structure:  <base64url(payload)>.<base64url(hmac-sha256)>
     """
     nonce = secrets.token_urlsafe(12)
-    payload_bytes = json.dumps({"uid": user_id, "n": nonce}, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
+    payload: Dict[str, Any] = {"uid": user_id, "n": nonce}
+    if flow == "invite" and invite_token:
+        payload["flow"] = "invite"
+        payload["invite_token"] = invite_token
+
+    payload_bytes = json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":")).encode()
     payload_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(payload_bytes).rstrip(b"=").decode()
 
     sig = hmac.new(
@@ -116,16 +121,16 @@ def _build_state(user_id: str) -> str:
     return f"{payload_b64}.{sig_b64}"
 
 
-def _decode_state(state: str) -> Tuple[str, bool]:
+def _decode_state(state: str) -> Tuple[Dict[str, Any], bool]:
     """
     Verify the state signature and return (user_id, is_valid).
 
-    Returns (user_id, True) on success, ("", False) on tampered/malformed state.
+    Returns (payload, True) on success, ({}, False) on tampered/malformed state.
     """
     try:
         payload_b64, sig_b64 = state.rsplit(".", 1)
     except ValueError:
-        return "", False
+        return {}, False
 
     # Verify HMAC
     expected_sig = hmac.new(
@@ -136,21 +141,33 @@ def _decode_state(state: str) -> Tuple[str, bool]:
     expected_b64 = base64.urlsafe_b64encode(expected_sig).rstrip(b"=").decode()
 
     if not hmac.compare_digest(expected_b64, sig_b64):
-        return "", False
+        return {}, False
 
     # Decode payload
     try:
         padding = "=" * (4 - len(payload_b64) % 4)
         payload = json.loads(base64.urlsafe_b64decode(payload_b64 + padding))
-        return str(payload["uid"]), True
+        if not isinstance(payload, dict) or not payload.get("uid"):
+            return {}, False
+        return payload, True
     except Exception:
-        return "", False
+        return {}, False
+
+
+def get_state_context(state: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:
+    payload, valid = _decode_state(state)
+    if not valid:
+        return {}
+    return {
+        "flow": payload.get("flow"),
+        "invite_token": payload.get("invite_token"),
+    }
 
 
 # â”€â”€ OAuth helpers â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€â”€
 
 
-def build_auth_url(user_id: str) -> QBConnectURLResponse:
+def build_auth_url(user_id: str, *, flow: Optional[str] = None, invite_token: Optional[str] = None) -> QBConnectURLResponse:
     """
     Build the Intuit OAuth 2.0 authorization URL for the given user.
 
@@ -158,7 +175,7 @@ def build_auth_url(user_id: str) -> QBConnectURLResponse:
     knows which user to bind the QB connection to without needing an extra
     query parameter (Intuit only forwards code, state, and realmId).
     """
-    state = _build_state(user_id)
+    state = _build_state(user_id, flow=flow, invite_token=invite_token)
     params = {
         "client_id": settings.quickbooks_client_id,
         "scope": " ".join(QB_SCOPES),
@@ -271,7 +288,7 @@ async def ensure_valid_token(conn: QBConnection) -> QBConnection:
 
 async def handle_oauth_callback(
     state: str, code: str, realm_id: str
-) -> QBConnection:
+) -> tuple[QBConnection, Dict[str, Any]]:
     """
     Exchange the authorization code for tokens and upsert a QBConnection.
 
@@ -281,7 +298,8 @@ async def handle_oauth_callback(
     Called from the OAuth callback endpoint after Intuit redirects the user back.
     If the user already has a QBConnection, it is replaced with the new tokens.
     """
-    user_id, valid = _decode_state(state)
+    state_payload, valid = _decode_state(state)
+    user_id = str(state_payload.get("uid", "")).strip()
     if not valid or not user_id:
         logger.warning(f"QB callback: invalid or tampered state token received.")
         raise HTTPException(
@@ -345,7 +363,11 @@ async def handle_oauth_callback(
         await conn.insert()
 
     logger.info("QB connection established.")
-    return conn
+    context: Dict[str, Any] = {
+        "flow": state_payload.get("flow"),
+        "invite_token": state_payload.get("invite_token"),
+    }
+    return conn, context
 
 
 async def get_connection_status(user_id: str) -> QBConnectionStatusResponse:
test_patch:
diff --git a/loangen-agent/tests/test_quickbooks_oauth_state.py b/loangen-agent/tests/test_quickbooks_oauth_state.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..7739bd0
--- /dev/null
+++ b/loangen-agent/tests/test_quickbooks_oauth_state.py
@@ -0,0 +1,184 @@
+"""Tests for QuickBooks OAuth invite-flow state propagation.
+
+The OAuth `state` token must be able to carry the invite context (a flow marker
+and the invite token) through the Intuit round-trip, and the callback must send
+the browser back to the right place. These tests exercise the observable,
+public behaviour only:
+
+  * ``build_auth_url`` (signed state construction)
+  * ``get_state_context`` (state -> invite context)
+  * the ``/oauth/callback`` route handler (redirect target)
+"""
+
+from __future__ import annotations
+
+import unittest
+from types import SimpleNamespace
+from unittest.mock import AsyncMock, patch
+from urllib.parse import parse_qsl, urlsplit
+
+
+class QBOAuthStateContextTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    def test_default_connect_has_no_invite_context(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1").state
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(state), {"flow": None, "invite_token": None})
+
+    def test_invite_flow_context_round_trips(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="tok-abc123").state
+        self.assertEqual(
+            get_state_context(state),
+            {"flow": "invite", "invite_token": "tok-abc123"},
+        )
+
+    def test_invite_flow_requires_a_token(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token=None).state
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(state), {"flow": None, "invite_token": None})
+
+    def test_empty_invite_token_carries_no_context(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        # An empty invite token is "no token" — a partial context must not be
+        # embedded any more than a missing one would be.
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="").state
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(state), {"flow": None, "invite_token": None})
+
+    def test_only_invite_flow_value_is_carried(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="default", invite_token="tok-xyz").state
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(state), {"flow": None, "invite_token": None})
+
+    def test_tampered_state_yields_no_context(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="tok-abc").state
+        tampered = state[:-2] + ("AA" if not state.endswith("AA") else "BB")
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(tampered), {})
+
+    def test_payload_tampered_state_yields_no_context(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        # Editing the payload (where the invite token lives) must break the
+        # signature — an attacker cannot forge or swap the invite context.
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="tok-abc").state
+        payload, signature = state.split(".", 1)
+        forged_payload = ("A" if payload[0] != "A" else "B") + payload[1:]
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context(forged_payload + "." + signature), {})
+
+    def test_malformed_state_yields_no_context(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import get_state_context
+
+        self.assertEqual(get_state_context("not-a-real-state-token"), {})
+
+    def test_auth_url_embeds_the_signed_state(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url, get_state_context
+
+        resp = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="tok-embed")
+        params = dict(parse_qsl(urlsplit(resp.auth_url).query))
+        self.assertEqual(params.get("state"), resp.state)
+        self.assertEqual(
+            get_state_context(params["state"]),
+            {"flow": "invite", "invite_token": "tok-embed"},
+        )
+
+
+class QBAuthUrlStableTests(unittest.TestCase):
+    """Behaviour that must hold regardless of the invite-flow work."""
+
+    def test_auth_url_carries_client_and_response_type(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url
+
+        resp = build_auth_url("user-1")
+        params = dict(parse_qsl(urlsplit(resp.auth_url).query))
+        self.assertEqual(params.get("response_type"), "code")
+        self.assertIn("client_id", params)
+        self.assertEqual(params.get("state"), resp.state)
+
+    def test_state_token_has_two_signed_parts(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1").state
+        self.assertEqual(len(state.split(".")), 2)
+        self.assertTrue(all(state.split(".")))
+
+    def test_distinct_users_get_distinct_state(self) -> None:
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url
+
+        self.assertNotEqual(
+            build_auth_url("user-a").state, build_auth_url("user-b").state
+        )
+
+
+class QBOAuthCallbackRedirectTests(unittest.IsolatedAsyncioTestCase):
+    async def _run_callback(self, *, invite_token, raises):
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks import router as qb_router
+        from agent.core.config import settings as app_settings
+
+        if invite_token is None:
+            state = build_auth_url("user-1").state
+        else:
+            state = build_auth_url(
+                "user-1", flow="invite", invite_token=invite_token
+            ).state
+
+        if raises:
+            fake_callback = AsyncMock(side_effect=RuntimeError("token exchange failed"))
+        else:
+            fake_callback = AsyncMock(
+                return_value=(
+                    SimpleNamespace(user_id="user-1"),
+                    {"flow": "invite", "invite_token": invite_token},
+                )
+            )
+
+        with patch.object(app_settings, "frontend_url", "https://app.example"):
+            with patch.object(qb_router, "handle_oauth_callback", new=fake_callback):
+                return await qb_router.qb_callback(
+                    code="auth-code", state=state, realm_id="realm-1"
+                )
+
+    async def test_invite_success_returns_to_invite_advisor(self) -> None:
+        resp = await self._run_callback(invite_token="tok-success", raises=False)
+        location = resp.headers["location"]
+        self.assertIn("/start/invite/tok-success/advisor", location)
+        self.assertIn("qb_success=1", location)
+
+    async def test_invite_failure_returns_to_invite_advisor(self) -> None:
+        resp = await self._run_callback(invite_token="tok-failure", raises=True)
+        location = resp.headers["location"]
+        self.assertIn("/start/invite/tok-failure/advisor", location)
+        self.assertIn("qb_error=1", location)
+
+    async def test_tampered_state_does_not_grant_invite_redirect(self) -> None:
+        # A state whose invite context fails verification must fall back to the
+        # generic redirect — a forged token cannot hijack the invite return page.
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks.service import build_auth_url
+        from agent.integrations.quickbooks import router as qb_router
+        from agent.core.config import settings as app_settings
+
+        state = build_auth_url("user-1", flow="invite", invite_token="tok-forged").state
+        payload, signature = state.split(".", 1)
+        forged = ("A" if payload[0] != "A" else "B") + payload[1:] + "." + signature
+
+        fake_callback = AsyncMock(
+            return_value=(SimpleNamespace(user_id="user-1"), {"flow": None, "invite_token": None})
+        )
+        with patch.object(app_settings, "frontend_url", "https://app.example"):
+            with patch.object(qb_router, "handle_oauth_callback", new=fake_callback):
+                resp = await qb_router.qb_callback(
+                    code="auth-code", state=forged, realm_id="realm-1"
+                )
+        location = resp.headers["location"]
+        self.assertNotIn("/start/invite/", location)
+        self.assertIn("qb_success=1", location)
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    unittest.main()
